metadata.title: Rabi Detuning Landscape with Claude
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: Olá. This is Liam, in for Bear. One Claude prompt. A live interactive simulation of p_flip(t) + lorentzian p_max(δ).
Quantum mechanics is full of non-obvious results. Today's: P_flip(t) + Lorentzian P_max(Δ). Key checkable number: Δ/Ω=1→P_max=0.5, FWHM=2Ω. Let us build the demonstration.

props.prompt: Build me an interactive D3 simulation of p_flip(t) + lorentzian p_max(δ). Use D3 version 7 from CDN. Single-file HTML, all physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Add at least one slider or control. Teardown palette: background #FFFFFF, ink #2A1A0E, accent #C8102E.

beats.narration_text: Claude returns a single-file HTML. D3 version 7. All the physics runs in the browser. No server. No build step.
Here is the baseline — the simulation at rest. P_flip(t) + Lorentzian P_max(Δ).
Now interact with the control — drag the slider, click the toggle. Watch the physics respond.
The simulation updates in real time. Δ/Ω=1→P_max=0.5, FWHM=2Ω. Exactly as the equations predict.
One prompt. A live quantum mechanics demonstration. P_flip(t) + Lorentzian P_max(Δ). Bear reviews these in the morning and ships the ones that pass.
Your turn. Extend this vol3 rabi detuning landscape simulation. Add a second control that changes [parameter]. Show how it affects the result.

props.prompt: Extend this vol3 rabi detuning landscape simulation. Add a second control that changes [parameter]. Show how it affects the result.
